name: ORACLE_HCM__LIST_WORKERS
description: List or search workers in Oracle HCM Cloud. Supports filtering by query parameters such as PersonNumber, WorkerId, DisplayName, and more. Returns a paginated list of worker records.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_WORKER
description: Retrieve the full worker record for a specific person using their Person ID. Use the 'expand' query parameter to include related personal details such as names, emails, phones, and addresses inline.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

PersonId.description: The unique Person ID of the worker in Oracle HCM (e.g., '300000012345678').

name: ORACLE_HCM__UPDATE_EMPLOYEE_ASSIGNMENT
description: Update an employee assignment (PATCH). Typically used to change working hours, FTE, assignment category, payroll basis, people group, or work schedule. Must include ActionCode and ReasonCode for auditability. Supply only the fields you want to change.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body. Use 'application/json' for standard requests or 'application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json' for ADF resource items.

PersonId.description: The unique Person ID of the employee.

AssignmentId.description: The unique Assignment ID to update.

body.description: JSON body containing assignment fields to update. Only fields to be changed need to be included.

NormalHours.description: Normal working hours per frequency period (e.g., 32 for 32 hours/week).

AssignmentCategory.description: Assignment category lookup code (e.g., 'FR' for full regular, 'PR' for part regular).

EffectiveStartDate.description: Effective start date of the assignment change in YYYY-MM-DD format.

EffectiveEndDate.description: Effective end date in YYYY-MM-DD format (usually '4712-12-31' for open-ended).

ActionCode.description: The HR action code driving the change (e.g., 'CHANGE_WORKING_CONDITIONS').

ReasonCode.description: The reason code for the action (e.g., 'EMPLOYEE_REQUEST').

name: ORACLE_HCM__LIST_EMPLOYEE_SALARIES
description: Retrieve current and historical salary records for a specific employee. Returns all salary entries including effective dates, salary amounts, and salary basis.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__CREATE_EMPLOYEE_SALARY
description: Create a new salary record for an employee. Typically used when a compensation change needs to reflect an assignment or working-hours change. Requires SalaryAmount, SalaryBasisId, and DateFrom at minimum.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body. Use 'application/json' for standard requests or 'application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json' for ADF resource items.

PersonId.description: The unique Person ID of the employee.

SalaryBasisId.description: Unique identifier of the salary basis (e.g., 300000012345678).

DateFrom.description: Start date for the new salary in YYYY-MM-DD format.

DateTo.description: End date for the salary in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use '4712-12-31' for open-ended.

AssignmentId.description: Optional Assignment ID the salary is tied to.

name: ORACLE_HCM__UPDATE_EMPLOYEE_SALARY
description: Update an existing salary record for an employee. Typically used to adjust salary amount or dates on an existing salary entry.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body. Use 'application/json' for standard requests or 'application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json' for ADF resource items.

PersonId.description: The unique Person ID of the employee.

SalaryId.description: The unique Salary ID to update.

body.description: JSON body containing salary fields to update.

SalaryBasisId.description: Unique identifier of the salary basis (e.g., 300000012345678).

DateFrom.description: Start date for the new salary in YYYY-MM-DD format.

DateTo.description: End date for the salary in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use '4712-12-31' for open-ended.

AssignmentId.description: Optional Assignment ID the salary is tied to.

name: ORACLE_HCM__LIST_DOCUMENT_RECORDS
description: List document records. Commonly filtered by PersonId via 'q=PersonId=...' to list documents associated with a specific employee.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__CREATE_DOCUMENT_RECORD
description: Create a new document record in the Document of Record (DOR) for an employee. Attachments are uploaded via a subsequent multipart/form-data POST to the document's attachments child resource.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body. Use 'application/json' for standard requests or 'application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json' for ADF resource items.

body.description: JSON body for the new document record.

PersonId.description: Person ID of the employee the document belongs to (e.g., 300000012345678).

DocumentCode.description: Unique business code for the document (e.g., 'WH_CONSENT').

DateFrom.description: Effective start date of the document in YYYY-MM-DD format.

DateTo.description: Effective end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Optional.

Country.description: Country code associated with the document (optional).

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_DOCUMENT_RECORD
description: Retrieve a specific document record by its Document ID.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__UPDATE_DOCUMENT_RECORD
description: Update metadata or version of an existing document record. Supply only fields to be changed.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body. Use 'application/json' for standard requests or 'application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json' for ADF resource items.

DocId.description: The unique Document Record ID to update.

body.description: JSON body containing document fields to update.

PersonId.description: Person ID of the employee the document belongs to (e.g., 300000012345678).

DocumentCode.description: Unique business code for the document (e.g., 'WH_CONSENT').

DateFrom.description: Effective start date of the document in YYYY-MM-DD format.

DateTo.description: Effective end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Optional.

Country.description: Country code associated with the document (optional).

name: ORACLE_HCM__EXECUTE_REPORT
description: Execute a BI Publisher report asynchronously. Returns an execution ID which can be polled for status and output. Commonly used for worklist extracts and data-heavy reports.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body. Use 'application/json' for standard requests or 'application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json' for ADF resource items.

ReportPath.description: Absolute path of the BI Publisher report (e.g., '/Custom/HR/Working_Hours_Change_Worklist.xdo').

ParameterNameValues.description: List of parameter name / value pairs to pass to the report.

Values.description: Array of string values to pass for the parameter (e.g., ['2026-04-20']).

Locale.description: Locale for the report execution (e.g., 'en-US'). Optional.

Attribute.description: Optional Attribute setting for the report.

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_REPORT_EXECUTION_STATUS
description: Check the execution status of a previously submitted BI Publisher report. Returns the current status (e.g., 'RUNNING', 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED') so the caller can poll until the report is ready.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

ExecutionId.description: The unique ID of the report execution.

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_REPORT_EXECUTION_OUTPUT
description: Download the output of a completed BI Publisher report execution. Response content depends on the output format requested at execution time (CSV, PDF, XLSX, etc.).

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

ExecutionId.description: The unique ID of the report execution.

name: ORACLE_HCM__IMPORT_AND_LOAD_DATA
description: Trigger an HCM Data Loader (HDL) batch import and load for a previously uploaded data file. Used as a fallback when multiple assignment fields need atomic update in a single transaction, or for large bulk operations. Returns an HDL Flow ID which can be polled for status.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body. Use 'application/json' for standard requests or 'application/vnd.oracle.adf.resourceitem+json' for ADF resource items.

ContentId.description: The Content ID of the previously uploaded HDL .zip file (returned from UCM file upload).

SuppressFileProcessing.description: Whether to suppress file processing after upload ('Y' or 'N'). Default 'N'.

SuppressAutoLoad.description: Whether to suppress automatic load after import ('Y' or 'N'). Default 'N'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__LIST_HDL_FLOW_STATUSES
description: List HCM Data Loader (HDL) batch flow statuses. Returns recent HDL flows along with their current processing state. Supports filtering with 'q' (e.g., 'ContentId=UCMFA0012345').

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_HDL_FLOW_STATUS
description: Retrieve the status and result of a specific HCM Data Loader (HDL) flow execution by Flow ID. Returns processing status, record counts, and any errors.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

FlowId.description: The unique ID of the HDL flow execution.

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_COMMON_LOOKUPS
description: Retrieve valid lookup values from the commonLookupsLOV list-of-values resource. Typically filtered with 'q=LookupType=<LOOKUP_TYPE>'. Common lookup types include: ASSIGNMENT_CATEGORY, PAYROLL_BASIS, FULL_PART_TIME, FREQUENCY.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName,DateOfBirth'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

LookupType.description: Convenience parameter equivalent to 'q=LookupType=<value>'. Common values: 'ASSIGNMENT_CATEGORY', 'PAYROLL_BASIS', 'FULL_PART_TIME', 'FREQUENCY'.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__LIST_BPM_TASKS
description: List BPM workflow tasks assigned to or associated with the authenticated user. Supports pagination and filtering to replace BI reports for high-frequency integrations. Useful for retrieving pending notifications and human-workflow work items. For HCM flows, pass applicationContext='hcm' to scope the list to HCM tasks; the returned identificationKey can then be used with ORACLE_HCM__LIST_HCM_TASKS (findByIdentificationKeys) to resolve back to worker/absence/time records.

applicationContext.description: Scope the task list to a specific application context. For HCM approval/notification flows, set to 'hcm' so that only HCM tasks assigned to the user are returned by default.

assignment.description: Filter tasks by assignment scope (Oracle-documented parameter). Typical values: 'MY', 'MY_AND_GROUP', 'GROUP', 'REPORTEES', 'ADMIN', 'ALL', 'PREVIOUS', 'REVIEWER'.

priority.description: Filter tasks by priority. Typical values: '1' (Highest) through '5' (Lowest).

identificationKey.description: If provided, only tasks with the given task identification key are returned. In HCM this is typically the HCM transaction ID, alert notification ID, absence request ID, or time record group ID.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of task attributes to include in the response. Include 'identificationKey' when you plan to resolve tasks back to HCM business objects via the HCM Tasks API.

keyword.description: Search keyword across task titles and payloads.

offset.description: Zero-based offset for pagination.

limit.description: Maximum number of tasks to return.

orderBy.description: Order the results by a specific attribute (e.g., 'createdDate:desc').

fromDate.description: Return tasks created on/after this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

toDate.description: Return tasks created on/before this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_BPM_TASK
description: Retrieve the full details of a specific BPM workflow task by Task ID. Returns task metadata, payload, history, and available actions (e.g., APPROVE, REJECT).

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

TaskId.description: The unique ID of the BPM task (e.g., '1400560').

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_BPM_RUNTIME
description: Retrieve the runtime capabilities and feature availability of the BPM engine for the current environment and security context. Called 'Retrieve Application Information' in Oracle's documentation. Does NOT execute business logic or start workflows.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__UPDATE_BPM_TASK
description: Act on a BPM workflow task (approve, reject, reassign, escalate, withdraw, etc.) by sending an action payload via PUT to the specific task. The set of valid actions is returned in the task's 'actionList' (e.g., 'APPROVE', 'REJECT', 'REASSIGN', 'ESCALATE', 'DELEGATE', 'SUSPEND', 'WITHDRAW', 'INFO_REQUEST'). Common HCM use case: approve or reject a resignation, transfer, or other transaction request from the worklist. The caller supplies the full nested JSON body exactly as Oracle expects — e.g., {"action":{"id":"APPROVE"},"comment":{"commentStr":"approved via API"}}.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Content-Type.description: Media type of the request body.

TaskId.description: The unique BPM task identifier. Can be a numeric task number (e.g., '1400560') or a task GUID (e.g., 'cbb10696-5c11-4d43-8e80-90f3d77c373a').

body.description: Free-form JSON body sent to Oracle's BPM Tasks API. Pass the full nested JSON exactly as Oracle expects. Minimum: {"action":{"id":"<ACTION_ID>"}}. With a comment: {"action":{"id":"APPROVE"},"comment":{"commentStr":"approved via API"}}. Valid action ids include: APPROVE, REJECT, REASSIGN, ESCALATE, SUSPEND, WITHDRAW, DELEGATE, INFO_REQUEST, SAVE, UPDATE_COMMENT, UPDATE_ATTACHMENT.

name: ORACLE_HCM__LIST_HCM_TASKS
description: List HCM task records and resolve them back to the underlying HCM business objects (worker, absence, time record, alert, etc.). Typically used as the second hop of the Worklist API flow: after calling ORACLE_HCM__LIST_BPM_TASKS with applicationContext='hcm', pass the returned identificationKeys to this endpoint using the 'findByIdentificationKeys' finder to get an 'AdditionalInformation' link for each task (pointing to publicWorkers, absences, timeRecordGroups, or back to the BPM Tasks API for alerts). Expand 'changedAttributes' to see the proposed vs. current field-level changes for Transaction Framework tasks.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'IdentificationKey,ModuleIdentifier,AdditionalInformation'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Use 'changedAttributes' to include field-level before/after values for Transaction Framework tasks.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'IdentificationKey:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Primary usage: 'findByIdentificationKeys;IdentificationKeys="<comma-separated keys>"' to resolve a batch of BPM identificationKeys (e.g., HCM transaction IDs, ALERT:<id>, absence IDs, time record group IDs) into HCM task details.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__LIST_PUBLIC_WORKERS
description: List or search public worker records (limited, public-view worker data) in Oracle HCM Cloud. Returned by the 'AdditionalInformation' link on worker-centric HCM tasks (e.g., Change Manager, ManagePerson). Supports filtering by query parameters such as PersonNumber, PersonId, DisplayName, and more.

q.description: Filter expression to search resources. Example: 'PersonNumber=12345' or 'DisplayName LIKE %John%'. Supports standard Oracle HCM finder syntax.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per page. Default 25. Maximum typically 500.

offset.description: Zero-based index of the first record to return (for pagination).

orderBy.description: Order results by one or more fields. Example: 'PersonNumber:asc' or 'LastName:asc,FirstName:asc'.

finder.description: Named finder to invoke on the resource. Example: 'PrimaryKey;PersonId=12345'.

totalResults.description: If true, includes the total count of records in the response.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

name: ORACLE_HCM__GET_PUBLIC_WORKER
description: Retrieve a specific public worker record by Person ID. This is the resource pointed to by the 'AdditionalInformation' link returned in ORACLE_HCM__LIST_HCM_TASKS for worker-centric tasks (e.g., Change Manager, ManagePerson). Use the 'expand' query parameter to include related child resources such as names, emails, phones, and addresses inline.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return in the response. Example: 'PersonId,PersonNumber,DisplayName'.

expand.description: Comma-separated list of child resources to expand inline. Example: 'names,emails,phones,addresses'.

onlyData.description: If true, returns only data without HATEOAS links. Default false.

links.description: Comma-separated list of relation names to include as HATEOAS links.

dependency.description: Attribute(s) to use when introducing finder dependency.

REST-Framework-Version.description: Oracle REST framework version. Recommended value: '7'.

Effective-Of.description: Effective date context for effective-dated data. Example: 'RangeMode=CURRENT' or 'RangeMode=EFFECTIVE;RangeDate=2026-05-01'.

PersonId.description: The unique Person ID of the worker (e.g., '100000008153768').
